meta.scenario: house B to A relative minor move, 5.5 BPM gap, mood darkening major to minor
meta.source: Kerri Chandler @ Maida Vale Essential Mix 2019-04-27

transition_arc: 6B (Bb major) to 6A (Ab minor) — moving from major to its relative minor. Mood darkening: bright major feel of T1 gives way to T2 minor groove. 5.5 BPM increase (117 to 123) adds subtle energy. T2 from_bar=0 has genuine percussion intro from bar 0. B to A relative move is one of the smoothest possible harmonic transitions.

annotated_actions.t1_state: T1 bar 8: groove active after 8-bar intro, bass+brass stabs+kick, RMS 0.48
T1 bar 64 (play bar 56 + from_bar 8): groove, drums=0.55, harmonic=0.50, rms=0.45
annotated_actions.t2_state: T2 bar 0: light kick + hi-hat (drums=0.4), bass=0.0, harmonic=0.1
annotated_actions.why: T1 at steady groove. B to A relative move allows harmonic blending at any energy level. Exit at bar 56 is a phrase boundary (7 x 8 bars).

annotated_actions.t2_state: T2 bar 0: genuine percussion intro — kick pattern active from bar 0, no bass
annotated_actions.why: T2 from_bar=0: clean intro with kick present. Relative key means other:0.6 harmonic stems are safe immediately (Ab minor shares all notes with Bb major). Mood shift starts gradually over these 16 bars.

annotated_actions.t1_state: T1 play bar 64: bass=0.22, drums=0.28, rms=0.28 — clearly diminishing
annotated_actions.why: Midpoint swap. T2 bass emerged at bar 8, T1 bass diminishing.

annotated_actions.t2_state: T2 bar 16: full minor groove, bass+kick+pads at 123 BPM, RMS 0.50
annotated_actions.why: T2 full groove. Mood shift from major T1 to minor T2 complete.
